prediction.reasoning: Stock prices are influenced by a wide range of factors, including market trends, economic indicators, company-specific news, and investor sentiment, all of which can be difficult to predict. Thus, these confidence intervals are based on historical volatility and assume normal market conditions. However, short-term stock movements can be unpredictable and actual prices can fall outside these intervals.
